chapter_title.en: Chapter on Performing Hajj on Behalf of a Slave Mother (umm Al-walad) if She Passes Away

text: : أَرْسَلْتُ إِلَى أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّ أُمَّ اِمْرَأَةٍ كَانَتْ أُمَّ وَلَدٍ فَمَاتَتْ فَأَرَادَتِ اَلْمَرْأَةُ أَنْ تَحُجَّ عَنْهَا قَالَ «أَ وَ لَيْسَ قَدْ عَتَقَتْ بِوَلَدِهَا تَحُجُّ عَنْهَا ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.2924 - Ibn Faddal narrated from Yunus ibn Ya'qub, who said:
I sent a message to Abu Abdullah (as) asking about the mother of a woman who was a concubine (umm walad). She had passed away, and the woman wanted to perform Hajj on her behalf.
Imam (as) said: "Did she not gain her freedom through her child? She may perform Hajj on her behalf."
